items.title: 10,000 jobs on offer at Perkeso carnival
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: A total of 10,000 job opportunities, with salaries ranging from RM3,000 to RM16,000, were offered at the two-day Social Security Organisation's (Perkeso) MYFutureJobs Premium Carnival 2026, which concluded today. Human Resources Minister Datuk Seri R. Ramanan said the carnival featured a wide range of employers, including multinational corporations, government-linked companies (GLCs) and private sector firms. "This carnival will also be held in Borneo, expanding access to employment nationwide," he said after visiting the MYFutureJobs Premium Career Carnival 2026 at the Kuala Lumpur Convention Centre today.

items.title: ‘True face of the Ministry of Health workforce’: Minister salutes Semporna mobile team for mid-sea rescue (VIDEO)
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, April 26 — Health Minister Datuk Seri Dzulkefly Ahmad today praised the Semporna Community Mobile Boat Clinic team for their swift and courageous rescue of two island residents whose boat capsized in Sabah waters last week. “Mr Azalan (Boat Crew): Without hesitation, he immediately jumped into the sea to rescue the victims,” Dzulkefly said in a Facebook post. “Mr Samsi & Mr Ansu: They not only helped secure the victims’ safety, but also saved the boat itself because for residents here, a boat is their lifeline and source of income.

items.title: Student injured after falling into seven‑metre well at Wakaf Bharu school in Kelantan
items.summary: KOTA BHARU, April 26 — A 17-year-old male student sustained head and body injuries after falling into a well more than seven metres deep at Sekolah Menengah Kebangsaan Agama Falahiah, Wakaf Bharu, here today. Wakaf Bharu Fire and Rescue Station (BBP) operations commander Azmi Sulaiman said the department received an emergency call at 9.12am, prompting six personnel and a Light Fire Rescue Tender (LFRT) to be dispatched to the scene. “The team arrived about 11 minutes later and found the victim inside the well.

items.title: Remand extended for independent preacher suspected of NGO funds abuse
items.summary: SEREMBAN: The Negri Sembilan Malaysian Anti-Corruption Commission (MACC) has obtained a remand extension against an independent preacher on suspicion of embezzling funds from a non-governmental organisation (NGO) totalling about RM12.3 million. The application to extend the remand was made after the original remand period for the suspect expired today. It was previously reported that the state MACC had arrested an independent preacher suspected of abusing power and embezzling funds from an NGO totalling about RM12.3 million for personal gain.

items.title: MACC secures remand extension for preacher in RM12.3m NGO funds probe
items.summary: SEREMBAN, April 26 — The Malaysian Anti-Corruption Commission (MACC) Negeri Sembilan has received approval to extend the remand of an independent preacher on suspicion of embezzling funds from a non-governmental organisation (NGO) totalling approximately RM12.3 million. The application to extend the remand was made after the original remand period for the suspect expired today. Previously, the state MACC had arrested an independent preacher suspected of abusing power and embezzling funds from an NGO totalling approximately RM12.3 million for personal gain.

items.title: Timeline: How the shooting at the White House correspondents' dinner unfolded
items.summary: Soon after, Secret Service agents rushed towards Trump, escorting the president and his cabinet from the ballroom. A sole gunman had rushed a Secret Service checkpoint in a lobby, Washington, DC, Mayor Muriel Bowser and interim police chief Jeffery Carroll later told reporters. As the suspect ran through that checkpoint, Secret Service members intercepted him.

items.title: MOH launches oral health drive, targets older adults to keep 20 teeth by 2030, says Dzulkefly
items.summary: KUALA SELANGOR, April 26 — The Ministry of Health (MOH) is targeting more than 50 per cent of senior citizens to retain at least 20 natural teeth by 2030, in line with the National Oral Health Strategic Plan 2022-2030. Health Minister Datuk Seri Dr Dzulkefly Ahmad said that based on the National Oral Health Survey of Adults (NOHSA) 2020, only 34.3 per cent of senior citizens have at least 20 natural teeth, while 13.9 per cent experience oral functional limitations that affect their quality of life. “I have stressed that there is no health without oral health, as it reflects the crucial role of dental health in maintaining overall well-being,” he said when met after launching the 6020 Campaign and the National-level Kembara Senyuman 2026 programme here today.

items.title: Police hunt mother over alleged abuse of three-year-old in Batu Caves
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Police are tracking down a woman suspected of abusing her three-year-old son in an incident in Batu Caves recently. Gombak police chief ACP Noor Ariffin Mohamad Nasir said a report was lodged yesterday by the child's grandmother, who lives in the same house. "The suspect is a local woman and the child's biological mother.

items.title: Police seek mother accused of abusing three‑year‑old son in Batu Caves
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, April 26 — Police are tracking down a woman suspected of abusing her three-year-old son in an incident in Batu Caves recently. Gombak police chief ACP Noor Ariffin Mohamad Nasir said a report was lodged yesterday by the child’s grandmother, who lives in the same house. “The suspect is a local woman and the child’s biological mother.

items.title: 'I've studied assassinations': Trump muses on reasons for latest shooting
items.summary: WASHINGTON: US President Donald Trump struck a philosophical tone Saturday after a shooter rushed a media gala in Washington, the latest in a series of incidents to target the Republican leader. a reporter asked Trump after the chaotic incident at the White House Correspondents' Dinner. "Well, you know, I've studied assassinations," Trump said.

items.title: What we know about the Trump press gala shooting
items.summary: WASHINGTON: US President Donald Trump and other attendees were evacuated Saturday night after gunshots were fired at a glitzy media gala in Washington. Authorities said no dignitaries or gala guests were hurt. How the shooting unfolded A "sole gunman" rushed through a security checkpoint in the hotel lobby just outside the ballroom where the dinner was taking place around 8:36 pm (0036 GMT), according to authorities.

items.title: Global supply crisis presents opportunities, Malaysia remains safe investment destination, says Tengku Zafrul
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, April 26 — Senior Political Adviser to the Prime Minister Tengku Datuk Seri Zafrul Abdul Aziz said the global supply crisis should be viewed as an opportunity to strengthen Malaysia’s position as a safe investment destination. He said Malaysia has a proven track record of economic resilience and a robust investment ecosystem in navigating various crises. Tengku Zafrul said Malaysia must continue positioning itself as a stable and safe investment destination.

items.title: Wira Transplant Walk: From dialysis to the summit, patients set sights on Mt Kinabalu climb to spotlight organ donation awareness (VIDEO)
items.summary: Patients on dialysis and organ recipients, alongside doctors and volunteers, are preparing to climb Mt Kinabalu to show that serious illness does not limit active lives. The initiative, Wira Transplant Walk, aims to raise awareness about organ donation and highlight the need for stronger national coordination and a transplant centre in Malaysia. “People still think transplant patients cannot live normally,” he said.

items.title: #NSTViral: Woman calls out diners for smoking in eatery [WATCH]
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: A viral video has surfaced showing a woman calling out several individuals for smoking and leaving cigarette ash on a restaurant table. In the 36-second clip shared by user @araaz_official on Threads, the woman is seen addressing the group while they remain silent. "Look at that cigarette ash.

items.title: Consumers are cautious as psychology of spending changes
items.summary: LETTERS: Malaysians are still spending, but the way they spend has changed. Consumers are therefore comparing more carefully, switching brands more easily and waiting longer for promotions. Consumers want visible value: better durability, better ingredients, better safety, better service, better convenience or a more trusted promise.

items.title: Pontian police seize 18 e-bikes used by foreigners on public roads
items.summary: PONTIAN: Police seized 18 micro-mobility vehicles, including electric motorcycles and bicycles, used by foreigners on public roads during a traffic crackdown on Friday. District police chief Superintendent Hadzrat Hussien Mion said micro-mobility vehicles were restricted from public roads under traffic laws due to safety concerns. Hadzrat said the operation followed public complaints and ongoing concerns over road safety involving e-bikes.

items.title: 'Get down!' Panic and chaos at glitzy media gala [WATCH]
items.summary: WASHINGTON: A glitzy gala for the White House Correspondents' Association was shattered by gunfire on Saturday night, forcing security to rush President Donald Trump offstage as guests dived for cover. Trump was seated on stage for the annual dinner — his first as president — when loud bangs disrupted the event. Trump said later he initially "thought it was a tray going down" before realising it was gunfire.

items.title: US Fed set to hold rates steady again on cost hikes from Mideast war
items.summary: WASHINGTON: The US central bank is widely expected to keep interest rates unchanged at its policy meeting next week, as energy prices stay high and supply chains snarled due to war in the Middle East. Powell's successor has faced a bumpy road to confirmation, while policymakers battle competing pressures as steeper fuel prices drive inflation and job market worries linger. Fed officials are set to keep rates steady at a range between 3.50 per cent and 3.75 per cent, extending their pause since the start of the year.

items.title: Trump says shooting won't 'deter' him from Iran war
items.summary: WASHINGTON: US President Donald Trump said Saturday a shooting at the White House correspondents dinner would not deter him from the Iran war, although he believed the incident was unlikely to be linked to the conflict. "It's not going to deter me from winning the war in Iran. The US leader earlier Saturday cancelled his envoys' trip to Pakistan for peace talks with Iran, after being unimpressed with Teheran's negotiating position following nearly two months of war.

items.title: Student, 17, rescued after falling into 7m-deep school well
items.summary: Wakaf Bharu Fire and Rescue Station operations commander Azmi Sulaiman said the department received an emergency call regarding the incident at 9.12am. "Upon arrival, they found the victim trapped inside the well and immediately initiated a rescue operation using specialised equipment, including ropes and a rescue ladder," he said. Azmi said firemen took more than 20 minutes to extricate the student from the well.

items.title: Six passengers injured evacuating from Swiss Air flight in India
items.summary: BENGALURU: Six passengers were injured and hospitalised after a Swiss Air flight from Delhi to Zurich aborted takeoff and was evacuated on the runway at the Indira Gandhi International Airport early Sunday, the Economic Times reported. The incident occurred when one of the aircraft's engines failed and caught fire during the takeoff roll, prompting the crew to stop the aircraft and initiate an emergency evacuation, the report said. © New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

items.title: #NSTviral: Hit-and-run caught on video in Jalan Templer traffic
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: A viral video has surfaced showing a hit-and-run incident along Jalan Templer involving a tour bus during heavy traffic yesterday. The 27-second clip, shared by Mynewshub+ on Facebook, shows a tour bus crashing into a silver Perodua Myvi before driving away. © New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

items.title: Sabah Rail to go cashless by June
items.summary: KOTA KINABALU, April 26 — Sabah State Railway (JKNS) is developing a cashless payment system for daily commuters, expected to be completed by June this year. Its director Mohammad Safri Abdul Habi said the initiative is part of the department’s digitalisation efforts, aimed at providing a more convenient and efficient payment method for rail users in the state. “JKNS is finalising the study.

items.title: Ex-judge can improve confidence in MACC says PM’s political adviser
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, April 26 — The appointment of former High Court Judge Datuk Seri Abdul Halim Aman to lead the Malaysian Anti-Corruption Commission (MACC) can strengthen the people’s confidence in the institution, said senior political adviser to the Prime Minister Tengku Datuk Seri Zafrul Tengku Abdul Aziz Commenting on the matter, Tengku Zafrul said Abdul Halim’s background as a former judge added value to efforts to strengthen the integrity of MACC. “I welcome this appointment. He said the appointment of individuals with integrity would help increase the public’s confidence in efforts to combat corruption.

items.title: As imports eat into Malaysia’s poultry’s feed, food waste and insects offer alternatives
items.summary: The high dependence on grain corn and soybeans not only puts pressure on production costs, but also makes the country’s food supply chain more fragile when faced with international crises. Animal nutrition expert, Prof Dr Hasliza Abu Hassim, said Malaysia currently does not have significant local corn and soybean production, causing the industry to continue to depend on imports. “Malaysia also basically does not have significant local corn and soybean production, so the dependence on imported feed materials remains high.

items.title: Fed-up traders call for protests to be held outside city centre
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: A group representing hawkers and petty traders has expressed frustration over rallies being held in business hotspots instead of open spaces. Federation of Malaysian Hawkers and Petty Traders Association president Datuk Seri Rosli Sulaiman said demonstrations in city centres lead to congestion and road closures. Rosli said.

items.title: Exposed: RM1,000 per head syndicate at Kedah border, senior immigration officer among 10 arrested
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, April 26 — Ten individuals, including a senior Immigration Department officer, were arrested in an operation dubbed Op Toyu at the Bukit Kayu Hitam Immigration, Customs, Quarantine and Security (ICQS) Complex in Kedah yesterday. Immigration director-general Datuk Zakaria Shaaban said the special operation, which began at 12.50 pm, was carried out following a month-long intelligence effort by the Intelligence and Special Operations Division at Immigration headquarters in Putrajaya, in collaboration with the Kedah Immigration Department and the Malaysian Border Control and Protection Agency (AKPS). “Also detained were five Indian nationals, one Pakistani national and a senior immigration officer believed to have acted as a ‘facilitator’,” he said in a statement today.

items.title: UUE Holdings RM536mil orderbook offers two-year earnings visibility
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: UUE Holdings Bhd's outstanding orderbook of RM536.4 million is expected to provide more than two years of earnings visibility, RHB Research said. The research house said the orderbook includes a recently secured RM16 million engineering, procurement, construction and commissioning contract. UUE Holdings reported fourth-quarter financial year 2026 (FY26) core net profit of RM7.3 million, bringing full-year core net profit to RM22.1 million.

items.title: Rising resin costs to weigh on Southern Cable
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Southern Cable Group Bhd is expected to face margin pressure in the second half of 2026 as rising polyethylene prices weigh on Malaysian cable manufacturers, Hong Leong Investment Bank Bhd (HLIB) said. The firm said Southern Cable's margins are likely to remain steady in the first half of the year, with any compression skewed towards the second half. HLIB said its sensitivity analysis showed that every one per cent increase in polyethylene prices would trim gross profit margin by about 0.06 percentage point.

items.title: Immigration officer among 10 held in 'Ops Toyu' smuggling syndicate bust
items.summary: ALOR STAR: A senior Immigration officer and a Thai agent were among 10 people detained following an operation at the Bukit Kayu Hitam Immigration, Customs, Quarantine and Security (ICQS) complex yesterday. The 12.50pm operation, dubbed "Ops Toyu", was conducted by a special task force from the Putrajaya Immigration Department's Intelligence and Operations Division, with support from Kedah Immigration and the Border Control and Protection Agency (MCBA). During the raid, authorities arrested the senior officer, the Thai agent, two local men, one of whom acted as a transporter, five Indian nationals, and one Pakistani man.

items.title: Immigration busts syndicate using layered transit route via Singapore, Thailand
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: The Immigration Department has busted an Indonesian migrant smuggling syndicate that used a complex, multi-layered transit route involving Singapore and Thailand to evade Malaysian authorities. In a statement, the department said the operation, conducted yesterday, led to the arrest of 10 Indonesian migrants and two transporters, aged between 27 and 60, at Terminal Bersepadu Gombak and Terminal Bersepadu Selatan. Migrants reportedly travelled from Indonesia to Singapore or via Kuala Lumpur International Airport as transit points before flying to Hat Yai, Thailand.

items.title: China FM tells Myanmar leader Beijing will back its security, sovereignty
items.summary: BEIJING: China's top diplomat Wang Yi said Beijing will "firmly support" Myanmar in safeguarding its national sovereignty and security, in a meeting with junta chief-turned-president Min Aung Hlaing. Beijing supports Myanmar "in forging a successful development path that aligns with its national conditions and enjoys the support of its people", Wang told Min Aung Hlaing during a visit to capital Naypyidaw, according to a Chinese foreign ministry readout published late Saturday. "As this year marks the first year of the new Myanmar government's tenure, both sides should seize this opportunity to carry forward and promote their traditional friendship, (and) open up new prospects for bilateral relations," Wang said.

items.title: Malaysia Smelting prospects bright on tin rally
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Malaysia Smelting Corp Bhd is poised for stronger earnings growth amid expectations of elevated tin prices and improving mining operations, Public Investment Bank Bhd (PublicInvest) said. Following a recent site visit to Malaysia Smelting's Rahman Hydraulic Tin (RHT) open-pit mine, the research house said optimism was also supported by steady gains in operational efficiency and recovery rates. Meanwhile, PublicInvest said the commissioning of the company's new tin tailing scavenging plant is set to lift daily tin concentrate production to 14 tonnes from 11 tonnes previously.

items.title: Malaysia could capture diverted Hormuz trade flows if logistics efficiency improves, academics say
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, April 26 — Malaysia is well-positioned to absorb any increase in redirected trade flows through the Strait of Melaka if shippers decide on alternative routes to avoid the conflict-stricken Strait of Hormuz. This is due to the country’s strong geostrategic advantage as a regional supply chain hub, said Universiti Kuala Lumpur professor Dr Nor Aida Abdul Rahman. “The record container volumes handled by Port Klang and Port of Tanjung Pelepas show that Malaysia already has a credible capacity base and market relevance,” she said.

items.title: Simultaneous Melaka, Johor polls could save millions, says analyst
items.summary: PUTRAJAYA: Holding the Melaka and Johor state elections simultaneously could save millions in taxpayer funds due to the proximity of the two states, an analyst said. Universiti Malaya political analyst Dr Mohammad Tawfik Yaakub said combined polls would allow for more organised logistics for both government machinery and political parties. This means the Melaka state election must be held by late February 2027, while Johor's must take place by June 2027, should both assemblies reach their full terms.

items.title: Loud bangs and a Trump evacuation: Chaos at correspondents' dinner
items.summary: IT was meant to be a glitzy Saturday night at a Washington ballroom featuring President Donald Trump, but the glamour was shattered by gunshots that left guests diving to the floor and the US leader bundled out by security personnel. Trump was seated on the dais at the White House Correspondents' Association's annual dinner – the first time he is attending as president – when loud bangs disrupted the revelry and caused him and others on stage to look up in alarm. Video captured by AFPTV, other news outlets, and AFP reporters attending the event showed a chaotic scene unfolding.

items.title: Man killed, wife and cousin injured in Tenom 4WD crash
items.summary: TENOM: A man was killed while his wife and cousin were injured after their four-wheel-drive vehicle skidded and overturned at Km7 of Jalan Tenom-Enubai near Kampung Sri Meranti last night. Both were rushed to Tenom Hospital for treatment. District police chief Superintendent Azmir Abd Razak said preliminary investigations revealed the Toyota Hilux was travelling from Kampung Bunsilon towards Tenom town.

items.title: New parties eye GRS after Usno departure
items.summary: KOTA KINABALU: Several political parties have applied to join Gabungan Rakyat Sabah (GRS), reflecting the state's dynamic political landscape, said its secretary-general, Datuk Seri Masidi Manjun. Masidi, who is also Sabah Deputy Chief Minister II and Finance Minister, said the decision by the United Sabah National Organisation (Usno) to leave GRS was part of the ever-changing and unpredictable political reality in the state. Let the GRS chairman (Datuk Seri Hajiji Noor) announce it at the appropriate time," he told reporters here on Friday.

items.title: #NSTviral: Mat Rempit swarm toll plaza in viral video
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: A group of motorcyclists turned a highway toll plaza into a scene of chaos early this morning, performing dangerous stunts and obstructing traffic. The footage depicts a large swarm of motorcyclists performing various stunts, including "wheelies", directly at the entrance of the plaza. © New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

items.title: Four-year-old left by mother melts Putra Perdana caregiver’s heart, adoption process now underway
items.summary: SEPANG, April 26 — A Selangor babysitter with three children of her own is in the process of adopting a four-year-old boy abandoned by his biological mother. Dg Che Nor Hakimah Mudah, in her 30s who lives in Putra Perdana, has been looking after the boy for more than two weeks, Harian Metro reported last night. “I contacted the child’s biological mother on April 10 and she agreed to let me adopt her child,” she was quoted as saying.

items.title: Johor Umno Youth calls for economic stimulus package amid energy crisis
items.summary: JOHOR BARU: Johor Umno Youth has urged the state government to introduce a comprehensive economic stimulus package to cushion the impact of the global energy crisis. State Umno Youth chief Noor Azleen Ambross said rising fuel costs were squeezing households and businesses across key sectors. Noor Azleen said the state must consider including single individuals under 40 in phase four of Bantuan Kasih Johor, a state financial aid programme for low- and middle-income groups.

items.title: Shooting suspect was 'would-be assassin,' says Trump [WATCH]
items.summary: WASHINGTON: US President Donald Trump said Saturday that the suspect behind a shooting incident at a media gala in Washington was a "would-be assassin," adding that the man was "armed with multiple weapons." The man charged past a security checkpoint, Trump told a press briefing at the White House, adding: "One officer was shot but saved by the fact that he was wearing a, obviously, a very good bulletproof vest." Multiple shots were fired at the White House correspondents dinner with president Trump in attendance.

items.title: Health Minister praises Semporna mobile clinic team for daring mid-sea rescue [WATCH]
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Health Minister Dr Dzulkefly Ahmad has lauded the bravery and quick thinking of a Semporna mobile clinic boat team that saved two islanders from drowning after their vessel capsized. In a Facebook post, Dzulkefly expressed his pride in the mobile clinic team's response. https://www.facebook.com/reel/1515983893264858 NST, News, Malaysia News, Region, Dr Dzulkefly Ahmad, Semporna, mobile clinic, Health Ministry, boat rescue, Sabah, healthcare access, Semporna Community Mobile Boat Clinic, heroics © New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

items.title: Melaka police move to charge 39-year-old after toddler allegedly tests positive for cannabis-laced sweets
items.summary: MELAKA, April 26 — A 39-year-old man is expected to face charges in court tomorrow for allegedly exposing a three-year-old girl to drug-laced sweets. Melaka police chief Datuk Dzulkhairi Mukhtar said instructions to charge the suspect have been received from the deputy public prosecutor (DPP), Harian Metro reported yesterday. The suspect, who reportedly has a large online following, was arrested on April 15 after the case came to light when the child was treated at Melaka Hospital in a semi-conscious state on April 14.

items.title: Usno split over GRS exit decision, deputy says move not unanimous
items.summary: KOTA KINABALU, April 26 — United Sabah National Organisation (Usno) Deputy President Datuk Ibrahim Linggam today said the decision to leave Gabungan Rakyat Sabah (GRS) was not a unanimous one. He described the decision by party president Tan Sri Pandikar Amin Mulia to withdraw from GRS as hasty and not reflective of the views of the party’s broader leadership. “Usno’s Supreme Executive Council comprises 37 members.

items.title: Azam Baki plans active retirement without politics after leaving MACC
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, April 26 — Outgoing Malaysian Anti-Corruption Commission (MACC) chief commissioner Tan Sri Azam Baki said he intends to remain physically active after stepping down next month, while brushing aside talk of a political future. Azam, whose contract ends on May 12, said he has yet to outline firm post-office plans but expects to keep up with regular exercise, The Star reported today. Azam at the same time called on MACC officers to remain united and fully support the incoming leadership as the agency transitions to a new chief commissioner.

items.title: ‘People will come and go’: Masidi says more applying to join GRS after Usno quits
items.summary: KOTA KINABALU, April 26 — Several political parties have applied to join Gabungan Rakyat Sabah (GRS), reflecting the state’s dynamic political landscape, said its secretary-general, Datuk Seri Masidi Manjun. Masidi, who is also Sabah Deputy Chief Minister II and Finance Minister, said the decision by the United Sabah National Organisation (Usno) to leave GRS was also part of the ever-changing and unpredictable political reality in the state. Meanwhile, GRS information chief Datuk Joniston Bangkuai, who was also present, said he respected Usno’s decision and described it as a normal occurrence in politics.

items.title: DAP sec-gen says party fully respects royals amid backlash over Selangor pig farm proposal remarks
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, April 26 — DAP has once again reaffirmed that it remains steadfast in its principle of holding the royal institution in the highest regard and has never compromised on that matter. DAP Secretary-General Anthony Loke Siew Fook said that this stance has always been the party’s foundation in ensuring that harmony between the royal institution and the country’s administrative system is preserved. I want to stress that DAP has always respected the royal institution and respects His Royal Highness the Sultan of Selangor,” he said.

items.title: Ex-judge Abdul Halim as new MACC boss will rebuild public trust, says govt backbenchers chief
items.summary: JOHOR BAHRU, April 26 — The appointment of former High Court judge, Datuk Seri Abdul Halim Aman as the new Malaysian Anti-Corruption Commission (MACC) Chief Commissioner is a strategic move to strengthen institutional reform and enhance public confidence in the integrity of the enforcement agency. Madani Government Backbenchers Club (BBC) chairman Datuk Seri Dr Zaliha Mustafa said the selection of a figure with a judicial background sends a clear signal that the Madani government is committed to ensuring the independence of the anti-corruption body. His Majesty Sultan Ibrahim, King of Malaysia, has consented to Abdul Halim’s appointment, which will take effect on May 13.

items.title: Seri Puteri Symphonic Winds Orchestra gets RM100,000 boost from Anwar ahead of Netherlands world contest
items.summary: PUTRAJAYA, April 26 — Prime Minister Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim tonight announced a contribution of RM100,000 to the Sekolah Seri Puteri (SSP) orchestra, known as the Seri Puteri Symphonic Winds Orchestra (SPWinds). Earlier, Anwar – who arrived at 8:00 pm accompanied by Ministry of Education Secretary-General Datuk Dr. Aminuddin Hassim – spent nearly two hours at the Plenary Hall, Putrajaya International Convention Centre (PICC), with approximately 1,000 spectators witnessing a high-energy collaboration between local artist Amir Masdi and the SPWinds orchestra. Organised by the SPWinds Parental Support Group (KSIB) under the Sekolah Seri Puteri Parent-Teacher Association (PIBG), the charity concert also aimed to raise funds for their upcoming trip to the Netherlands.

items.title: RM230m scandal: MACC extends remand on NGO deputy chief, company director till April 30
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, April 26 — The Malaysian Anti-Corruption Commission (MACC) Selangor has obtained an order to extend the remand of a deputy president of a non-governmental organisation (NGO) and a company director to assist in investigations into the misappropriation of approximately RM230 million in funds. Meanwhile, Selangor MACC director Mohd Azwan Ramli, when contacted, confirmed the development and said the case is being investigated under the MACC Act 2009. MACC had earlier confirmed that the case involved the misappropriation of public donations and not zakat funds.

items.title: The goodest girl: Meet Tessy, the German Shepherd sniffing out MRT cable thieves one theft at a time (VIDEO)
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, April 26 — Tessy may look like any other trained sniffer dog at first glance, but along the Klang Valley’s MRT rail alignments, her keen sense of smell has become a quiet yet formidable ally against a persistent and evolving threat. According to her owner, Yew Wai Cheong, Tessy was not initially trained to sniff out concealed cable-cutting tools or track down thieves in hiding who might otherwise evade human detection. Yew said he adopted Tessy from a good friend in 2021 and has served as his personal protection dog since.

items.title: At ‘Tangkap Azam Baki’ rally, protesters call for RCI, say fireworks were ‘sabotage’
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, April 25 — The fireworks that were briefly lit during the “Tangkap Azam Baki” rally was an act of sabotage, activists and political leaders said today. About 30 minutes into the rally, fireworks were set off near the main entrance of the Sogo shopping mall. Protesters gather as they march towards Dataran Merdeka during the 'Tangkap Azam Baki' rally.

items.title: MACC identifies 40 witnesses in Selangor NGO probe
items.summary: KUALA PILAH, April 25 — The Malaysian Anti-Corruption Commission (MACC) has identified 40 witnesses in its investigation into the alleged misappropriation of funds involving a non-governmental organisation (NGO) in Selangor. MACC Chief Commissioner Tan Sri Azam Baki said three individuals have been arrested so far, with two remanded, while statements have been recorded from 11 witnesses. "MACC has seized immovable assets estimated at RM27 million, involving four plots of land, seven houses, three office premises, 18 vehicles and jewellery.

items.title: ‘We hope for a miracle’: Family holds hope for Malaysian sentenced in Japan to be returned home
items.summary: According to the New Straits Times (NST), the family’s appointed lawyer Datuk Noor Svetlana Mohd Noor Nordin confirmed the sentence was for three charges, with the imprisonment running concurrently from his arrest date on November 18,2025. According to Noor Svetlana, the family plans to file a notice of appeal to the High Court. Faiq’s father Mohd Jailani had attended the hearing and sentencing alongside Noor Svetlana.

items.title: Cops investigating fireworks during ‘Tangkap Azam Baki’ rally, unexploded ones found at otherwise ‘safe’ rally that drew 300 protesters
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, April 25 — The police are investigating an incident where fireworks were set off during the Tangkap Azam Baki rally here, confirming that a bag of unlit fireworks was found at the scene. Dang Wangi police chief ACP Sazalee Adam said the rally was carried out “peacefully and safely”, with about 300 protesters present this afternoon. The crowd reacts fireworks are set off by unknown persons during Tangkap Azam Baki Rally here at Jalan Raja Kuala Lumpur, April 25, 2026.

items.title: PM Anwar’s digital divide warning: ‘Technology must benefit everyone’
items.summary: BUKIT MERTAJAM, April 25 —The nation’s digital transformation agenda must benefit the wider population and not be confined to a select group, particularly urban and high-income communities, Prime Minister Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim said. He said the government is determined to prevent a digital divide between affluent urban groups and rural communities with limited access to technology. Also present were Chief Minister Chow Kon Yeow, Communications Minister Datuk Fahmi Fadzil, Chief Secretary to the Government Tan Sri Shamsul Azri Abdul Bakar, Communications Ministry secretary-general Datuk Abdul Halim Hamzah and MCMC chairman Tan Sri Mohamad Salim Fateh Din.

items.title: Anwar: Abdul Halim free from business and political links, right choice to lead MACC
items.summary: BATU KAWAN, April 25 — Prime Minister Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim today explained the selection of former judge Datuk Seri Abdul Halim Aman as the new chief commissioner of the Malaysian Anti-Corruption Commission (MACC), touting his judicial background and lack of political or business ties as key qualifications for the role. Speaking at the Road Transport Department’s (JPJ) 80th Anniversary Celebration here, Anwar also confirmed that Abdul Halim will be appointed for a two-year term starting May 13. He said Abdul Halim’s judicial background makes him a suitable candidate to lead the anti-graft agency at a critical time, stressing that the new chief is not linked to the world of business or politics, which is seen as an effort to restore public confidence and signal a new chapter for the commission.

items.title: New MACC chief must probe ‘corporate mafia’ claims, says DAP’s Gobind
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, April 25 — DAP national chairman Gobind Singh Deo today challenged the incoming Malaysian Anti-Corruption Commission (MACC) chief to investigate allegations of a “corporate mafia” within the agency and resolve the shareholding controversy involving his predecessor, Tan Sri Azam Baki. The call comes after the government announced today the appointment of Datuk Seri Abdul Halim Aman as the new MACC chief commissioner, effective May 13. Gobind also stressed the need to conclude the investigation into the share ownership controversy involving the outgoing chief, Azam Baki.

items.title: Penang development allocation up 60pc since 2022, says Anwar
items.summary: BATU KAWAN, April 25 — Development allocation for Penang has increased by up to 60 per cent compared with 2022, says Prime Minister Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim. “So in 2023, Penang was allocated RM5.7 billion, 2024 (RM5.8 billion), 2025 (RM7.6 billion) and for this year, RM7.8 billion. Anwar, who is also the Finance Minister, said among the key projects implemented in Penang were the Light Rail Transit (LRT) Mutiara Line project and the Traffic Dispersal Project between Juru Toll Plaza and Sungai Dua Toll Plaza (PTJSD).
